untranslatable_reason: This signal requires cross-market implied volatility (IV) comparison, gamma/vega analysis, and volatility spread construction. None of these are available in the allowed indicator set. The signal fundamentally operates on: (1) multi-market IV levels and spreads (not available), (2) Greek ratios like gamma and vega (not available), (3) fundamental/technical vol opinion assessment (not mechanically encoded). Single-market indicators like hvol_20 (realized volatility) cannot substitute for implied volatility structures or cross-market comparisons. This is a multi-leg options strategy requiring external IV surface data.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_163_0 is a volatility arbitrage/relative value signal requiring access to implied volatility data across multiple markets/underlyings and options Greeks. The allowed indicator set contains only single-market technical indicators (price, volume, RSI, ATR, etc.) and one volatility proxy (hvol_20 = realized volatility). To translate this signal would require: india_vix or similar IV indices for multiple assets, cross-market spread calculations, gamma/vega position monitoring, and directional vol opinion triggers—none of which are mechanically expressible with the available tools.
